data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

The bead is too big when any of these holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate top-level deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass and favor child-bead scoping before implementation.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children may split once more, and third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (focused title, description, AC; copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'` so the decomposition is visible.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260508T000329-82d80680/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing each child ID and slice, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a successful attempt. Do not mix partial implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260508T000329-82d80680/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

If you write `.ddx/executions/20260508T000329-82d80680/no_changes_rationale.txt`, it MUST carry one of:

- `verification_command: <one-line shell command>` — the loop runs it (cwd = project root, 60s); exit 0 closes the bead, non-zero rejects.
- `status: needs_investigation` + `reason: <why>` — bead stays open for triage.

Bare rationales ("nothing to do") are rejected.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260508T000329-82d80680/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260508T000329-82d80680/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Bead overrides project defaults

The bead description and AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and conservative project defaults (YAGNI, DOWITYTD, no-docs-unless-asked) in this worktree — write whatever the bead asks for.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.

## Decomposition depth cap
This bead is already at decomposition depth 2. Do not create another child layer; if it is still too large, reject the split with a short explanation and write no_changes_rationale.txt instead.

  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-11f0cbf2">
    <title>test: stabilize shared agent/cmd execution gates</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
Several otherwise scoped beads cannot be retried or closed because their required verification gates fail in shared agent/cmd execution tests unrelated to the bead under attempt. The queue currently parks those beads as needs-investigation even when their focused tests pass.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:969-971 calls excludeCleanupMetadataFromWorktreeGit(wtPath) after worktree creation and wraps failures as `excluding execute-bead cleanup metadata: ...`.
- cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead_test.go:90-109 fakeExecuteBeadGit.WorktreeAdd creates an ordinary directory and copies tracker/docs files, but does not initialize a git repository in the fake worktree. Cmd tests that call ExecuteBeadWithConfig now hit the real cleanup-metadata exclusion path against a non-git directory and fail with exit status 128.
- cli/cmd/agent_execute_loop_test.go:40 and cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead_e2e_test.go:32 are named in failed queue attempts as part of the red cmd execute-bead coverage.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_cancel_test.go:166 defines TestCancelLatency_UnderSLA; recent failed attempts report this test timing out after 10m with the worker stuck in cancellation/probe loops, blocking broader `go test ./internal/agent/...` gates.

PROPOSED FIX
- Make cmd fake execute-bead worktrees compatible with the production cleanup-metadata exclusion path, either by initializing enough git metadata in fakeExecuteBeadGit.WorktreeAdd or by injecting/seaming the exclusion operation for tests while keeping production behavior covered.
- Stabilize TestCancelLatency_UnderSLA so it deterministically observes cancellation and returns within its SLA timeout under package/race test runs.
- Re-run the specific cmd and agent tests that blocked the parked beads, then the broader package gates used by those beads.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change candidate-cycle, review-gate, lifecycle-hook, or production reachability behavior.
- Do not weaken production cleanup metadata exclusion; tests may use a seam only if production behavior remains covered by an integration test.
- Do not close or mark the dependent beads complete in this bead; it only restores the shared verification floor.

PARENT + DEPS
Parent: ddx-a9d130d0. No deps.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. TestInvokeExecuteBeadFromLoopParsesJSONAmidWarnings passes without `excluding execute-bead cleanup metadata: exit status 128`.
2. TestExecuteBeadContextBudgetFromConfig passes without `excluding execute-bead cleanup metadata: exit status 128`.
3. TestExecuteBeadRoutingEvidencePersisted, TestExecuteBeadRoutingEvidenceProviderFallsBackToHarness, and TestExecuteBeadRoutingEvidenceNoEvidence pass or are updated to exercise the corrected test worktree/exclusion path.
4. TestCancelLatency_UnderSLA passes repeatedly with `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run TestCancelLatency_UnderSLA -count=10 -timeout 30s`.
5. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... -run "TestInvokeExecuteBeadFromLoopParsesJSONAmidWarnings|TestExecuteBeadContextBudgetFromConfig|TestExecuteBeadRoutingEvidence|TestCancelLatency_UnderSLA" -count=1 passes.
6. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... passes.
7. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>phase:2, area:test, area:agent, area:cmd, kind:bug, reliability, prevention, spec:FEAT-010</labels>
    <metadata parent="ddx-a9d130d0" spec-id="FEAT-010" base-rev="0fc9c9dad78056c7be3704fc98560867790b7067" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260508T000329-82d80680"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <ref id="FEAT-010" path="docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md">Feature: Task Execution</ref>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

data.message: ddx-11f0cbf2 # 1     +3s tool `{command="git log --oneline -5 2>&1", description="Show recent commits"}` done 173ms

data.output:
Both fixes are committed:
1. **`cli/internal/agent/runner_test.go`** (checkpoint 6538e31): `TestRunLMStudioDispatchesThroughEmbeddedAgent` and `TestRunOpenRouterDispatchesThroughEmbeddedAgent` — added `t.Setenv("HOME", t.TempDir())`, `WorkDir: t.TempDir()`, and `context.WithTimeout(5s)` to prevent fizeau from loading the real global provider config and making TCP connections.

2. **`cli/cmd/try_test.go`** (f61c325): `TestTry_FlagsPlumbThrough` — added `t.Setenv("HOME", t.TempDir())` and `factory.AgentRunnerOverride = &tryHookRunnerStub{t: t}` so the pre-dispatch lint hook routes through a stub runner instead of creating a real fizeau service.

Root cause in both cases: `dispatchViaResolvedConfig` creates a fizeau service via `NewServiceFromWorkDir` when both `svc` and `runner` are nil. Fizeau loads `~/.config/fizeau/config.yaml` (which has real providers in this environment), then attempts TCP connections that block the test.
